excluded: a noisy-hardware robustness or auditability conclusion before Stage 138 reaches a terminal supported/not-supported decision
provider-wide or transformer-scale superiority beyond the matched fixed-width evidence gates
supported: an explicit post-collection rerun sequence from Stage 115 collection through Stage 138 objective claim gating
Stage 134 intake counters and missing-job counts must prove runner output readiness before Stage 113
a deterministic no-claim boundary when any downstream collection, assembly, calibration, metric, intake, or claim gate is blocked
a terminal decision distinction between replicated PhaseWrap advantage supported and not supported by the preregistered rule

gate_records.name: provider result collector handoff
gate_records.purpose: collect every Stage 114 provider/window shard into the Stage 113 provider_job_results.jsonl input

gate_records.name: runner result intake alignment
gate_records.purpose: confirm Stage 133 runner output paths and Stage 115 collector shards are aligned and ready

gate_records.name: job result evidence assembler
gate_records.purpose: assemble provider job counts into the declared Stage 107 evidence files used by Stage 109

gate_records.name: known-state calibration gate
gate_records.purpose: verify bitstring order and known-state calibration before interpreting packet counts

gate_records.name: robustness metric recomputation
gate_records.purpose: recompute preregistered lower-MAE score errors for PhaseWrap and every comparator family

gate_records.name: auditability metric preregistration
gate_records.purpose: bind any auditability wording to complete packet trace coverage and component reconstruction metrics

gate_records.name: auditability metric evaluator
gate_records.purpose: evaluate component reconstruction auditability metrics from calibrated provider packet counts

gate_records.name: first-provider statistical interpretation gate
gate_records.purpose: enforce Stage 146 shot-noise and Stage 147 calibration-confidence guardrails before advantage wording

gate_records.name: window evidence intake validator
gate_records.purpose: verify every independent window has calibration evidence, packet counts, and Stage 103 outputs

gate_records.name: replicated advantage claim gate
gate_records.purpose: apply the final all-window replicated advantage rule before any robustness conclusion

gate_records.name: objective claim gate
gate_records.purpose: combine terminal robustness and auditability branches into final objective wording

next_gate: Clear the first blocked gate in order. No noisy-hardware robustness or auditability advantage conclusion is allowed until Stage 138 reaches a terminal supported/not-supported decision after Stage 115, Stage 134, Stage 113, Stage 101, Stage 103, Stage 136, Stage 137, Stage 148, Stage 109, and Stage 110 are ready.
objective: Determine whether PhaseWrap-RoPE's compact phase-wrap positional score has measurable robustness or auditability advantages on noisy quantum hardware, compared with matched positional-score encodings, under fixed circuit width.
